Order these morning activities:
1. Eat breakfast
2. Wake up
3. Get dressed
4. Brush teeth
Painting takes 3 hours. Start at 1:00 PM. Done?
The ______ neighbor brought us ______ when we moved in.
Which is OPINION?
choices: Reading every day will change your life
questions.passageText: Reading every day will change your life. It builds vocabulary. It improves focus. It makes you a better writer. Studies show that students who read just 20 minutes a day score significantly higher on tests. So put down the phone, pick up a book, and read! You'll be glad you did.

questions.question: How does Jake feel when he first sees the papers?
questions.passageText: Jake walked into class and froze. There was a piece of paper on every desk. "You all studied last night, right?" Mrs. Hayes said cheerfully. Jake's stomach dropped. He had been at his cousin's birthday all evening — he hadn't even opened his book. He glanced at his best friend Ravi, who gave him a sympathetic shrug. As Mrs. Hayes handed out the test, Jake saw the topic at the top: World War II. They had spent two weeks on this. He had taken notes in class every day. Maybe — just maybe — he could do this. He picked up his pencil.

questions.question: Volume of rectangular box length 6, width 5, height 4?
What can you infer is happening to Grandma?
choices: She's trying to teach Sara to be lazy
questions.explanation: "Back doesn't like the work" suggests aging body.
questions.passageText: Every Saturday for as long as Sara could remember, she had visited her grandmother's garden. They would weed, water, and pick vegetables together. Grandma always seemed to know exactly what each plant needed — when it needed water, when it had a bug problem, when it was ready to harvest. "You'll learn," Grandma would say with a smile. This Saturday, Grandma sat in a chair instead of kneeling in the dirt. "My back doesn't like the work anymore," she said. "Sara, would you do the watering today?" Sara nodded and picked up the watering can, feeling something heavy in her chest.

questions.question: The ______ instruction manual was ______ to read.

wrongExplanations.1: Detailed could be hard or easy.

questions.question: Which word does NOT belong?
questions.explanation: First three are WEAPONS (or shield is for defense). Hammer is a TOOL.

wrongExplanations.1: Shield is for defense in battle — fits.

questions.question: He gave the book to ______.

12, 9, 6, 3, ___
Which is OPINION?
choices: The tongue has about 10,000 taste buds
Surprisingly, the tongue does not have separate zones
The tongue can detect five basic tastes
Umami is one of the tastes
questions.passageText: The tongue is one of the strongest muscles in the human body, but it does much more than help you taste. It also helps you speak, swallow, and clean your teeth. Your tongue has about 10,000 taste buds, which can detect five basic tastes: sweet, salty, sour, bitter, and umami. Surprisingly, the tongue does not have separate zones for different tastes — every taste bud can detect every flavor.

questions.question:
What is the 6th term?
2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ___
Right angle measure?

What comes next?
1, 4, 7, 10, 13, ___
Which is OPINION?
choices: Each soda has 10 teaspoons of sugar
Sugary soda has no place in schools
questions.passageText: Sugary soda has no place in schools. Each can contains 10 teaspoons of sugar. Studies show that students who drink soda gain weight, have more cavities, and feel less focused in class. We don't allow alcohol or candy bars in vending machines — why allow soda? Schools must ban soda for the health of our students.
questions.passageTitle: Soda Should Be Banned in Schools

choices: Cooking saves money and teaches you about nutrition.

questions.question: The ______ change in weather caught everyone off guard.
SMOKE : FIRE ::
questions.explanation: SMOKE comes from FIRE (effect from cause). SOUND comes from BELL. Effect to cause.

wrongExplanations.0: Rain comes from clouds — but it's a closer match for sound:bell.
wrongExplanations.1: Wind moves a tree — wind isn't FROM a tree.
wrongExplanations.3: Light comes from sun, not moon (moon reflects).

questions.question: What can you infer about Mei?
choices: She is thoughtful and willing to sacrifice for family
questions.explanation: Walking 45 minutes alone vs. calling shows sacrifice.
questions.passageText: Mei stared at the empty bus stop. 5:47 PM. The last bus was supposed to come at 5:30. She pulled out her phone — battery at 4%. Calling her mom would use the last of the battery, but walking home alone would take 45 minutes. Mei thought about how her mom had been working three jobs since Dad got laid off. Calling now meant Mom leaving work to pick her up — losing wages they couldn't afford to lose. Mei tightened her backpack strap and started walking, taking the route under the streetlights. By the time she reached her front door, her feet ached and her phone had died. "Why didn't you call?" her mom asked. Mei just smiled and said the bus was running late.

questions.question: What does Aunt Joan probably NOT realize?
choices: Mom didn't really want guests right now
questions.explanation: She's cheerful and oblivious to Mom's stress.

wrongExplanations.3: She came to visit Sara's family.

questions.passageText: Aunt Joan stood at the door with two big suitcases and a wide smile. "I'm here for the weekend!" she announced. Mom managed a smile, but Sara could see her clenching her jaw. Their guest room was full of boxes from moving. The kitchen was a mess. Mom hadn't slept well in days. Aunt Joan walked past Mom and into the kitchen. "Oh, you've been busy!" she chirped. "Don't worry, I'll fix everything up while I'm here."

Why did Marcus feel "suddenly calm"?
choices: The pressure of winning was lifted
questions.explanation: Win or lose, the coach is proud — pressure off.

wrongExplanations.0: OPPOSITE — calm is focus, not surrender.

questions.passageText: Coach Rivera watched the scoreboard. Down by two with 30 seconds left. He called a timeout. The team huddled around him, sweating, breathing hard. "I want everyone to know," he said quietly, "that no matter what happens in the next 30 seconds, I'm proud of every one of you. You've worked harder than any team I've coached." Marcus looked up. He had expected a strategy talk. Instead, he felt suddenly calm. The team broke the huddle and walked back onto the court.

wrongExplanations.3: Cloud is in the sky — part of.
